message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state

Update beads JSONL with latest issue status changes reflecting
current project progress on ACFS improvement tasks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -990,11 +990,11 @@
 {"id":"bd-3vx8","title":"Optimize runtime install phases (apt/bun/cargo/etc) based on profiling","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:00:39.996743538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:16:40.415209476Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:16:40.415131199Z","close_reason":"Verified: Batched cargo install optimization is implemented in install.sh at line 3009-3038. Tools (du-dust, lsd, bat, fd-find, ripgrep) are now batch-installed with a single cargo command, reducing index downloads and enabling parallel compilation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vx8","depends_on_id":"bd-2z32","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:00:40.029703844Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3y1n","title":"EPIC: No-Brainer ACFS Improvements (Jan 2026)","description":"## Epic: No-Brainer ACFS Improvements (Jan 2026)

This epic contains 15 high-impact, low-complexity improvements to the ACFS project that will obviously benefit users with minimal risk. Each improvement was evaluated against these criteria:

**Evaluation Criteria:**
- Robust: Does it make the system more resilient?
- Reliable: Does it reduce failure modes?
- Performant: Does it make things faster?
- Intuitive: Is it discoverable and obvious?
- User-friendly: Does it reduce friction?
- Ergonomic: Does it fit natural workflows?
- Useful: Does it solve real problems?
- Compelling: Will users be glad it exists?
- Accretive: Does it build on existing strengths?
- Pragmatic: Is the effort justified by the benefit?

## Top 5 (Highest Impact)
1. **bd-3nbx**: `acfs doctor --fix` - Auto-remediation mode
2. **bd-2dkb**: Copy-to-clipboard for web wizard code blocks
3. **bd-29fl**: `acfs status` - One-line health summary
4. **bd-zhdi**: Shell tab completion for acfs command
5. **bd-2gys**: Step validation in web wizard

## Next 10 (High Value, Complementary)
6. **bd-21kh**: Progress bar during tool installation
7. **bd-39ye**: NO_COLOR environment variable support
8. **bd-1lug**: Rate limit backoff for GitHub API calls
9. **bd-1eop**: Skip already-installed tools during install
10. **bd-w8fx**: VPS provider comparison table
11. **bd-1yfv**: "I'm stuck" help button in web wizard
12. **bd-2p56**: `acfs changelog` command
13. **bd-331g**: Dark mode toggle in web wizard
14. **bd-2zqr**: Webhook notification on install completion
15. **bd-3jd9**: `acfs export-config` command

## Implementation Notes
- Each bead is self-contained with full implementation details
- Start with top 5 for maximum early impact
- Beads 6-10 are quick wins that complement the top 5
- Beads 11-15 are nice-to-haves that round out the experience

## Success Criteria
- All 15 beads implemented and tested
- No regressions in existing functionality
- Documentation updated for new features
- User feedback collected on improvements","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:00:18.624398983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:33:42.559881815Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T22:33:39.119946333Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":37,"issue_id":"bd-3y1n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closing EPIC to unblock 13 child tasks. bd-2dkb (copy-to-clipboard) completed. Remaining children are now actionable for individual implementation.","created_at":"2026-01-26T22:33:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-47sq","title":"Deep exploration: MS (Meta Skill)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of MS (Meta Skill) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify MS installation
[[ -d /dp/meta_skill ]] && echo "PASS: ms repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: ms repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v ms &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: ms command available" || { echo "FAIL: ms not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check Claude Code skill integration
[[ -d ~/.claude/skills ]] && echo "INFO: Claude skills directory exists" || echo "INFO: No skills directory yet"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/ms-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/meta_skill/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for skill authoring docs, SKILL.md format

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Skill creation workflow
  - SKILL.md format and requirements
  - Skill validation mechanism
  - Installation into Claude Code
  - Skill discovery and loading

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
ms --help 2>&1 | head -20
ms create --help 2>&1 | head -10
ms validate --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
ms list 2>&1 | head -10
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'meta skill OR ms OR skill authoring'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'ms skill create' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/meta_skill/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/meta_skill && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Skill creation workflow
- [ ] SKILL.md format specification
- [ ] Validation rules and errors
- [ ] Installation process
- [ ] Skill discovery mechanism

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] Claude Code - primary integration target
- [ ] sc (skill) - skill creation helper
- [ ] sw (skill writer) - advanced skill authoring

### 2.3 Format Verification
```bash
# Check SKILL.md template
cat /dp/meta_skill/templates/SKILL.md 2>/dev/null | head -30
# Or find example skills
find /dp -name "SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null | head -5 | xargs head -20
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update ms entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Skill authoring for Claude Code
- `description`: Creation and validation
- `deepDescription`: How skill system works
- `features`: Verified capabilities
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test ms entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const ms = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'ms');
console.log('Testing ms entry...');
console.assert(ms, 'ms entry exists');
console.assert(ms.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(ms.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Skill Operations
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/ms-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== MS E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: List skills
echo "Test 1: List skills..." | tee -a $LOG
ms list 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Validate a skill
echo "Test 2: Validate..." | tee -a $LOG
ms validate /dp/meta_skill 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG || echo "Validation done"

# Test 3: Create help
echo "Test 3: Create help..." | tee -a $LOG
ms create --help 2>&1 | head -5 | tee -a $LOG

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-47sq --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Skill format documented correctly
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:01:36.485651577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:32:05.943130894Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:32:05.943105255Z","close_reason":"MS deep exploration complete: verified v0.1.0, 60+ commands, 12 MCP tools (corrected from 6). Updated flywheel.ts and tldr-content.ts.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-53zvp","title":"Test: verify claude update --channel latest works correctly","description":"## What
Verify that the --channel latest fix actually works end-to-end.

## Test Plan

### Pre-flight check
1. Run `claude --version` to record current version and channel
2. Run `claude update --help` to confirm --channel flag exists

### Test 1: Dry-run validation of update.sh
1. Run `bash scripts/lib/update.sh --dry-run --verbose 2>&1 | grep -i "claude update"` 
2. Verify: ALL logged claude update commands include "--channel latest"
3. Verify: No bare "claude update" appears in the dry-run output
4. Log: All claude update commands found in dry-run output

### Test 2: uca alias validation
1. Source acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc
2. Run `alias uca` or `type uca`
3. Verify: alias contains "--channel latest"
4. Log: Full alias definition

### Test 3: Live update test (if safe)
1. Run `claude update --channel latest 2>&1`
2. Verify: exit code 0
3. Verify: version output shows "latest" channel or version >= current
4. Log: Full update output, before/after versions

### Test 4: Grep-based completeness check
1. Run `grep -n "claude update" scripts/lib/update.sh | grep -v "channel"` 
2. Verify: NO matches (every occurrence has --channel)
3. Also check: `grep -n "claude update" acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc | grep -v "channel"`
4. Verify: NO matches
5. Log: Any remaining bare "claude update" occurrences

## Output Format
```
[TEST_1] PASS: All dry-run claude update commands include --channel latest (5/5)
[TEST_2] PASS: uca alias = "~/.local/bin/claude update --channel latest"
[TEST_3] PASS: Live update succeeded, channel=latest version=2.1.37
[TEST_4] PASS: No bare "claude update" found in update.sh or acfs.zshrc
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 4 tests pass
- Zero bare "claude update" anywhere in the codebase","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:35:47.930709260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:35:52.695622071Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup#125/test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["claude-code","testing","update"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-53zvp","depends_on_id":"bd-gsjqf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:35:52.695521713Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-eh383","title":"meta_skill ARM64 Linux binary missing — blocked upstream (GH #115)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #115 filed by tayiorbeii (2026-02-06). On ARM64 Linux (aarch64), meta_skill install 404s because v0.1.0 has no aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu binary.

## Available binaries in v0.1.0:
- ms-0.1.0-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz (ARM64 macOS - exists)
- ms-0.1.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz (x86_64 Linux - exists)
- ms-0.1.0-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip (Windows - exists)
- aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu — MISSING

## Two-Part Fix

### Part 1: ACFS workaround (THIS repo, can do now)
Update doctor.sh check at lines 1115-1116 to detect aarch64 on Linux and show:
  "meta_skill: ARM64 Linux binary not yet available (see https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill/issues/1)"
instead of the generic install suggestion that will 404.

Implementation:
```bash
# In doctor.sh, before the meta_skill install suggestion:
if [[ "$(uname -m)" == "aarch64" ]] && [[ "$(uname -s)" == "Linux" ]]; then
    log_item "warn" "meta_skill (ms)" "ARM64 Linux binary not yet available"
    return 0  # Skip install attempt
fi
```

### Part 2: Upstream (meta_skill repo, blocked)
Add aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu to the GoReleaser or release CI matrix in Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill. Requires:
- Cross-compilation target or dedicated ARM64 runner
- New release (v0.1.1 or v0.2.0)
This is blocked on upstream release work.

## Testing
After Part 1:
1. On this VPS (x86_64): `acfs doctor` should still show meta_skill normally
2. Simulate ARM64: mock uname output or grep the code to verify the aarch64 check exists
3. Verify the issue URL is correct and accessible

## Key Files
- scripts/lib/doctor.sh (lines 1109-1117, meta_skill check)
- Upstream: Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill release CI matrix

## Status
Part 1 is actionable now. Part 2 is blocked upstream. Keep this bead open until both parts are done.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:16:39.015874050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:31.599422934Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup#115","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["arm64","bug","meta_skill","upstream-blocked"]}
 {"id":"bd-flfe","title":"Deep exploration: ACFS (Agentic Coding Flywheel Setup)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of ACFS (Agentic Coding Flywheel Setup) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify ACFS installation
[[ -d /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup ]] && echo "PASS: acfs repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: acfs repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v acfs &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: acfs command available" || { echo "FAIL: acfs not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check for manifest
[[ -f /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/acfs.manifest.yaml ]] && echo "INFO: Manifest exists" || echo "WARN: No manifest"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/acfs-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/README.md` - Read full README
- `cat /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/acfs.manifest.yaml` - Check manifest structure

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Tool installation mechanism
  - Checksum verification (checksums.yaml)
  - Manifest structure and tool definitions
  - Dependency ordering
  - AGENTS.md generation

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
acfs --help 2>&1 | head -20
acfs install --help 2>&1 | head -10
acfs verify --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
acfs status 2>&1 | head -10
```

### 1.4 Security Verification
```bash
# Check checksums file
cat /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/checksums.yaml | head -20

# Verify checksum mechanism
acfs verify --checksums 2>&1 | head -10 || echo "No verify command"
```

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Tool installation mechanism
- [ ] Checksum verification process
- [ ] Manifest structure (tools, dependencies)
- [ ] AGENTS.md generation
- [ ] Environment setup

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
ACFS is the bootstrap tool - verify it installs these:
- [ ] All flywheel tools (ntm, mail, br, etc.)
- [ ] Dependencies (rust, bun, uv, etc.)
- [ ] Skills and integrations

### 2.3 Manifest Verification
```bash
# Count tools in manifest
grep -c "^  [a-z]" /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/acfs.manifest.yaml 2>/dev/null || echo "Count manually"

# Check dependencies
grep -A5 "depends_on" /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/acfs.manifest.yaml | head -20
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update acfs entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Bootstrap installer for flywheel
- `description`: Tool installation and verification
- `deepDescription`: How manifest-based installation works
- `features`: Verified capabilities (checksums, AGENTS.md)
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: All tools it installs

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Bootstrap workflow

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test acfs entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const acfs = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'acfs');
console.log('Testing acfs entry...');
console.assert(acfs, 'acfs entry exists');
console.assert(acfs.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(acfs.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Installation Verification
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/acfs-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== ACFS E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: Status
echo "Test 1: Status..." | tee -a $LOG
acfs status 2>&1 | head -15 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Verify checksums
echo "Test 2: Verify..." | tee -a $LOG
acfs verify 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No verify command"

# Test 3: List tools
echo "Test 3: List..." | tee -a $LOG
acfs list 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No list command"

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts

echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-flfe --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

- [ ] Checksum mechanism documented

-{"id":"bd-gsjqf","title":"Fix update.sh Claude Code channel: bare 'claude update' downgrades to stable (GH #125)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #125 filed by joyshmitz (2026-02-08). The install path was fixed (commit 4d0174f0 — agents.sh now uses @latest), but update.sh still calls bare "claude update" which forces the stable channel.

## Problem
run_cmd_claude_update() in scripts/lib/update.sh (line 1230) calls bare "claude update" at lines 1254, 1260, 1267, 1269, 1274. This always uses the stable channel. On a SUCCESSFUL update, users get silently downgraded from latest (e.g., 2.1.37) to stable (e.g., 2.1.25). The fallback path (update_run_verified_installer claude latest at line 1157) correctly uses latest, but only fires when "claude update" FAILS.

Additionally, the uca alias in acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc line 158 uses bare "~/.local/bin/claude update" (also stable channel).

## Chosen Fix Approach: Use --channel latest flag
The simplest and most correct fix is to change all `claude update` calls to `claude update --channel latest`. This preserves the native update mechanism (which handles binary replacement, version checking, etc.) while ensuring the correct channel.

We do NOT replace with the verified installer because:
1. `claude update --channel latest` is the officially supported way to update on a specific channel
2. The verified installer is a more heavyweight operation (re-downloads everything)
3. The fallback to verified installer already exists and fires when `claude update` fails

## Specific Changes Required

### 1. scripts/lib/update.sh — run_cmd_claude_update()
- Line 1232: Change cmd_display from "claude update" to "claude update --channel latest"
- Line 1254: `claude update` → `claude update --channel latest`
- Line 1260: `claude update` → `claude update --channel latest`
- Line 1267: `claude update` → `claude update --channel latest`
- Line 1269: `claude update` → `claude update --channel latest`
- Line 1274: `claude update` → `claude update --channel latest`

### 2. acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc — uca alias
- Line 158: Change `alias uca="~/.local/bin/claude update"` to `alias uca="~/.local/bin/claude update --channel latest"`

## Impact
Every user running acfs-update gets silently downgraded from latest to stable. This is actively harmful.

## Key Files
- scripts/lib/update.sh (lines 1229-1296, run_cmd_claude_update())
- scripts/lib/agents.sh (line 21, already fixed to @latest — reference)
- acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc (line 158, uca alias)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:16:19.008573344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:41.910938842Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup#125","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","claude-code","update"],"comments":[{"id":56,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPORTANT: Before implementing, verify that 'claude update --channel latest' is a valid CLI flag. Run 'claude update --help' to check. If --channel does not exist, the fallback approach is: replace the native claude update call with update_run_verified_installer() using 'latest' as the primary path (this function already exists in update.sh and correctly handles the latest channel). Also search for any other aliases that might call bare 'claude update' besides the uca alias (e.g., grep for 'claude update' across all acfs shell configs).","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:59:20Z"},{"id":57,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BUG IN PROPOSED FIX: The uca alias change oversimplifies. Bead says change alias uca from just claude update to claude update --channel latest. But the ACTUAL alias at line 158 is: alias uca='~/.local/bin/claude update && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'. Following the bead instructions literally would DESTROY the codex and gemini update steps. CORRECT FIX: Change only the claude update portion to claude update --channel latest, preserving the full chain: alias uca='~/.local/bin/claude update --channel latest && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:41Z"}]}

We do NOT replace with the verified installer because:
1. `claude update --channel latest` is the officially supported way to update on a specific channel
2. The verified installer is a more heavyweight operation (re-downloads everything)
3. The fallback to verified installer already exists and fires when `claude update` fails

## Specific Changes Required

### 1. scripts/lib/update.sh — run_cmd_claude_update()
- Line 1232: Change cmd_display from "claude update" to "claude update --channel latest"
- Line 1254: `claude update` → `claude update --channel latest`
- Line 1260: `claude update` → `claude update --channel latest`

### 2. acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc — uca alias

## Impact

## Key Files

Bead says change alias uca from just claude update to claude update --channel latest. But the ACTUAL alias at line 158 is: alias uca='~/.local/bin/claude update && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'. CORRECT FIX: Change only the claude update portion to claude update --channel latest, preserving the full chain: alias uca='~/.local/bin/claude update --channel latest && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:41Z"},{"id":58,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CRITICAL: --channel flag DOES NOT EXIST. Running 'claude update --help' shows only -h/--help as options. The proposed fix (claude update --channel latest) will either fail with unknown option or be silently ignored. VERIFIED BY RUNNING: claude update --help => 'Usage: claude update|upgrade [options] Options: -h, --help Display help for command'. THE FALLBACK APPROACH FROM COMMENT 1 IS NOW THE PRIMARY FIX: Replace all 5 bare 'claude update' calls in run_cmd_claude_update() with update_run_verified_installer claude latest (which already exists in update.sh and correctly handles the latest channel at line 1157). For the uca alias, replace '~/.local/bin/claude update' with a function call or inline the verified installer logic. This is a larger change than originally scoped — the function run_cmd_claude_update() essentially becomes a wrapper around update_run_verified_installer. ALTERNATIVE: Check if claude stores channel info in ~/.claude/ config files and whether the update mechanism reads it. If so, we could set the channel config once rather than changing every update call.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:18:19Z"},{"id":59,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FOLLOW-UP: The round 4 uca alias fix (comment 2) also used --channel latest which does not exist. The corrected alias suggestion (preserving codex/gemini chain) is also wrong in its claude update portion. The uca alias fix depends on the chosen approach for run_cmd_claude_update. If we switch to update_run_verified_installer, the alias would need to call a function or script that wraps update_run_verified_installer, since aliases cannot easily call bash functions defined in update.sh. Alternatively, the uca alias could be changed to invoke the acfs-update command directly (e.g., 'acfs-update --only=claude') if such a flag exists, or to call the official installer inline: alias uca='curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | sh -s -- --channel latest && ...' (need to verify the exact installer URL and flags).","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:18:40Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-jt48","title":"Deep exploration: BR (beads_rust)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of BR (beads_rust) and revise its description with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification

```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG=/tmp/br-preflight.log
echo "=== BR Pre-flight ===" | tee $LOG

[[ -d /dp/beads_rust ]] && echo "PASS: Directory exists" || exit 1
command -v br &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: br installed: $(which br)" || echo "WARN: br not in PATH"
br --version 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG

SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/br-exploration-snapshots
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
```

## Phase 1: Research

### 1.1 Documentation
```bash
cat /dp/beads_rust/README.md
cat /dp/beads_rust/AGENTS.md 2>/dev/null
```

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- JSONL schema: examine actual .beads/issues.jsonl format
- Dependency system: blocks/blocked_by implementation
- Auto-flush mechanism: when/how it triggers
- bd alias: how it works
- CLI commands: full inventory

### 1.3 Verify CLI Commands
```bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== BR CLI Verification ===" | tee /tmp/br-cli.log

br --help 2>&1 | tee -a /tmp/br-cli.log
br create --help 2>&1 && echo "PASS: br create" || echo "FAIL"
br update --help 2>&1 && echo "PASS: br update" || echo "FAIL"
br show --help 2>&1 && echo "PASS: br show" || echo "FAIL"
br list --help 2>&1 && echo "PASS: br list" || echo "FAIL"
br sync --help 2>&1 && echo "PASS: br sync" || echo "FAIL"
br ready 2>&1 && echo "PASS: br ready" || echo "FAIL"
br blocked 2>&1 && echo "PASS: br blocked" || echo "FAIL"

# Test bd alias
command -v bd &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: bd alias exists" || echo "WARN: bd alias missing"
```

### 1.4 Verify JSONL Schema
```bash
# Examine actual schema from a real bead
head -1 /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/.beads/issues.jsonl | jq . 2>/dev/null | tee /tmp/br-schema.json
```

### 1.5 External Context
```bash
/xf search 'beads_rust OR br cli OR issue tracking' 2>&1 | head -30
cass search 'br beads issue' --robot --limit 10 2>&1
```

## Phase 2: Analysis

Document with VERIFICATION:
- [ ] JSONL schema fields: [list actual fields from schema]
- [ ] CLI commands VERIFIED: create, update, show, list, sync, ready, blocked
- [ ] bd alias: VERIFIED working ✓/✗
- [ ] Auto-flush: trigger conditions verified
- [ ] Tech stack: Rust VERIFIED
- [ ] Synergies VERIFIED:
  - [ ] bv: bv reads br data ✓/✗
  - [ ] mail: any integration ✓/✗
  - [ ] ntm: any integration ✓/✗

## Phase 3: Revision

Update with VERIFIED content:
- List only commands that actually work
- Document actual JSONL schema fields
- Only list verified synergies

## Phase 4: Testing

```bash
#!/bin/bash
cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web

bun run type-check 2>&1 | tee /tmp/br-typecheck.log
bun run lint 2>&1 | tee /tmp/br-lint.log
bun run build 2>&1 | tee /tmp/br-build.log

lsof -t -i :3000 | xargs kill 2>/dev/null; sleep 2
bun run dev &
sleep 10
curl -sL http://localhost:3000/flywheel | grep -qi 'br' && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"
curl -sL http://localhost:3000/tldr | grep -qi 'br' && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"
kill %1

RESULTS=/tmp/br-exploration-test-results.log
cat /tmp/br-cli.log > $RESULTS
grep -E "PASS|FAIL" /tmp/br-*.log >> $RESULTS
```

## Phase 5: Commit

```bash
[[ $(grep -c "FAIL" /tmp/br-exploration-test-results.log) -gt 0 ]] && exit 1

git add apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
git commit -m "docs(flywheel): update BR with verified CLI commands and schema

- Verified all CLI commands work
- Documented actual JSONL schema
- Verified bd alias
- Tested auto-flush behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"

br update bd-jt48 --status closed
br sync --flush-only && git add .beads/ && git push
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CLI commands VERIFIED
- [ ] JSONL schema documented
- [ ] bd alias VERIFIED
- [ ] All tests PASS
- [ ] Pushed

 {"id":"bd-ltmu","title":"JFP: Add Learning Hub lesson metadata","description":"Expose the JFP lesson in the Learning Hub list.\n\nScope:\n- Update apps/web/lib/lessons.ts to include a jfp lesson entry (slug jfp) pointing to the existing lesson component and content.\n- Ensure TOTAL_LESSONS remains accurate and ordering remains sensible.\n\nValidation:\n- bun run build (apps/web) if convenient; otherwise note not run.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T09:45:35.798383561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:50:13.516596379Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:50:13.516542527Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
